title: wormia coromandelina
intro: Wormia coromandelina, also known as the Indian snakeroot, is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and snake bites. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which may provide health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, and boosting the immune system. Wormia coromandelina can be prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is sometimes used in herbal remedies in combination with other plants to enhance its medicinal effects.
